title: Chapter 6 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Chapter 6 concepts!

questions.question: What is an array used for?
options: To store multiple values in a single variable
questions.explanation: Arrays group related data items into a single, indexed list variable.

questions.question: Which represents a correctly declared array of 3 integers?
questions.explanation: The basic format is `type name[size] = {values};`

questions.question: In C++, the starting index of an array is _____.
questions.explanation: Arrays are 0-indexed. The first item is at index 0.

questions.question: If you access array element arr[4], you are getting the 4th item in the list.
questions.explanation: Since numbering starts at 0, arr[4] is the 5th item.

questions.question: Given `string names[3] = {"Alice", "Bob", "Eve"};` How do you change "Bob" to "Carl"?
questions.explanation: Bob is the second element, which sits at index 1. So, names[1] = "Carl"; overwrites it.

questions.question: What is the best way to iterate through all elements in an array cleanly without using an index counter?
questions.explanation: Foreach loops intelligently iterate through all items without needing a manual counter.

questions.question: To find how many characters are in a string, use the _____ function.
questions.explanation: The .length() function returns the character count. .size() is also valid.

questions.question: A string variable is secretly an array of characters.
questions.explanation: Yes, strings manage memory as an array of characters under the hood.

questions.question: What does this output? string word = "Apple"; cout << word[2];
questions.explanation: Indices: [0] is A, [1] is p, [2] is p.

questions.question: What symbol is used as the 'escape character' in C++ strings?
questions.explanation: The backslash `\` escapes special characters.

questions.question: How do you declare a string containing a double quote inside it?
questions.explanation: You must escape the inner quotes using \" so C++ doesn't confusingly terminate the full string.

questions.question: The shorthand `\n` creates a new _____ in a string.
questions.explanation: It acts like hitting the 'enter' key on a keyboard.

questions.question: When changing a character in a string using indexed brackets, you must provide a double-quote string like `word[0] = "H";`.
questions.explanation: You are replacing one char, so you MUST use single quotes `word[0] = 'H';`.

questions.question: If you have `int arr[5];`, valid index ranges are ________.
questions.explanation: Since it holds 5 elements and numbering starts at 0, the indices are 0, 1, 2, 3, 4.

questions.question: Which of these is the syntax for a for-each loop in C++ using an array called 'myNumbers'?
questions.explanation: The syntax is for (type variableName : arrayName) { }.

questions.question: The elements of an array are enclosed in _____ braces when initializing it directly.
questions.explanation: You populate the array using {};

questions.question: Arrays can hold a mix of different data types (e.g., an int and a string in the same array).
questions.explanation: In standard C++, an array can only hold values of the exact data type you declared (e.g., all integers or all strings).

questions.question: Why is looping through an array with a `for` loop beneficial?
options: It prevents us from having to manually type out arr[0], arr[1], arr[2]... saving a huge amount of code.
It is required by the language.
questions.explanation: Using the loop variable `i` dynamically queries the array for every item effortlessly.

questions.question: What is another built-in function that gets the size of a string, exactly like `.length()`?
questions.explanation: .size() is just an alias for .length(). They return the same thing.

questions.question: What happens if you try to access an index that is larger than the array's size (e.g., calling arr[10] on an array holding 3 items)?
options: You get unpredictable results (garbage data) or a memory crash.
questions.explanation: C++ will look at that unmanaged memory location, creating buggy behavior or program crashes.
